behavior: Diurnal, social omnivore that commonly forages on the ground; extended family groups may cooperate in rearing young, and large communal roosts can form outside the breeding season.
field_marks: Large, all-black corvid with a robust, moderately sized black bill.
Adult plumage is glossy black, often showing a subtle blue-violet sheen in good light.
Dark eyes, black legs, and strong black feet.
Broad wings show five conspicuous, fingered primary tips in flight.
Tail is short and square-tipped to slightly rounded, not wedge-shaped.
Usually flies with steady, methodical wingbeats.
habitat: Adaptable in open or semi-open places with trees and reliable food, including woodlands, forest edges, fields, farmland, shorelines, wetlands, and human-modified green spaces; generally avoids extensive unbroken forest and deserts.
